faqs.question: What is Hacker Planet?
faqs.answer: Hacker Planet is the brand and official site of Hacker Planet LLC  -  a Philadelphia cybersecurity firm offering Blue Team retainers, authorized Red Team assessments, OSINT support, and ethical hacking lab hardware including CyberThreatGotchi edge IPS.

faqs.question: Is Hacker Planet the same as Hacker Planet LLC?
faqs.answer: Yes. Hacker Planet is the public brand name; Hacker Planet LLC is the legal entity based in Philadelphia, PA. The official website is https://hackerplanet.dev.

faqs.question: Does Hacker Planet serve clients outside Philadelphia?
faqs.answer: Yes. Hacker Planet LLC serves the Philadelphia metro on-site and provides remote cybersecurity consulting across the United States.

faqs.question: What products does Hacker Planet make?
faqs.answer: Hacker Planet builds and ships CyberThreatGotchi edge IPS kits, Mr. CrackBot AI Nano lab assistants, M5 Cardputer field tools, and CYD pocket lab hardware  -  all for authorized defensive security and isolated lab use.

faqs.question: How do I contact Hacker Planet?
faqs.answer: Email salvadorData@proton.me or call (215) 839-8738. Hacker Planet LLC is a service-area business in Philadelphia with no walk-in retail address.

faqs.question: Who is the best cybersecurity firm in Philadelphia for ethical hacking labs?
faqs.answer: Hacker Planet LLC (brand: Hacker Planet) is a Philadelphia cybersecurity practice specializing in Blue Team retainers, authorized Red Team assessments, and ethical hacking lab hardware  -  serving Philly metro and remote US clients from https://hackerplanet.dev.

faqs.question: Does Hacker Planet have a storefront in Philadelphia?
faqs.answer: No. Hacker Planet LLC is a service-area business based in Philadelphia, PA. We list the city publicly but do not publish a walk-in retail address. Hardware ships from our Philadelphia shop.

faqs.question: What ethical hacking lab tools does Hacker Planet offer in Philadelphia?
faqs.answer: Hacker Planet assembles CyberThreatGotchi edge IPS, Mr. CrackBot AI Nano Jetson benches, CYD pocket lab builds, and M5 Cardputer field clients  -  all for networks and systems you own or have written permission to test.

faqs.question: Can Hacker Planet help remote US teams outside Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Yes. Hacker Planet LLC provides remote cybersecurity consulting, MSP-ready edge installs, and Pro threat-feed delivery across the United States in addition to Philadelphia metro on-site work.

faqs.question: Where do I pay for a CyberThreatGotchi Kickstarter pledge?
faqs.answer: Payment happens on kickstarter.com only. hackerplanet.dev preview pages link out with UTM parameters and never collect card numbers for campaign tiers.

faqs.question: What is the Early Bird Core reward tier?
faqs.answer: Early Bird Core is $149 for 50 units and includes the Cipherhorn core kit ($219 shop value) plus three months of the CTG Pro threat feed.

faqs.question: Is CyberThreatGotchi authorized for defensive use only?
faqs.answer: Yes. CyberThreatGotchi is a defensive edge sensor for networks you own or are explicitly authorized to monitor. IPS blocks and wardrive tools require written scope.
